release_context.label: Latest monthly labor-market releases available as of July 21, 2026
release_context.note: Values are public, seasonally adjusted headline indicators from BLS series as displayed by FRED. Data may be revised.

indicators.label: Unemployment rate
indicators.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED
indicators.interpretation: Headline unemployment fell to 4.2, keeping the labor market tighter.

indicators.label: Labor force participation
indicators.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED
indicators.interpretation: Participation fell to 61.5, tightening worker availability.

indicators.label: Total nonfarm payrolls
indicators.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED
indicators.interpretation: Payroll employment increased by 57 thousand to 158,984, a positive hiring signal.

indicators.label: Job openings
indicators.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED
indicators.interpretation: Openings rose by 9 thousand to 7,594, a labor-demand signal to compare with hiring and quits.

indicators.label: U-6 labor underutilization
indicators.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED
indicators.interpretation: Broader labor underutilization eased to 7.9, but still captures marginal attachment and involuntary part-time work.

indicators.label: Production and nonsupervisory hourly earnings
indicators.source_name: U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics via FRED
indicators.interpretation: Hourly pay for production and nonsupervisory workers rose to $32.38, a worker-pay gauge to read alongside inflation and hours.

sources.name: BLS Job Openings and Labor Turnover Survey

future_api.contract_note: Keep ids, source fields, periods, units, and release metadata stable before exposing paid agent access.
